headline: Broadcom miss sparks fears around AI margin pressure, investor says
summary: STORY: Wall Street advanced on Thursday but disappointing results from Broadcom led a chip selloff that held the Nasdaq's gains in check.Broadcom's revenue expectations sent its shares tumbling and cast a pall over the AI frenzy, which has sent chip stocks soaring so far this year."Traders, investors in the markets are anticipating potentially falling margins in the future because of the shift in Broadcom's business," said Rathbun. "Now, this actually has implications for the rest of AI."Rathbun also said rising expenses tied to infrastructure, partnerships and raw materials could eventually create margin pressure if companies are not able to pass those costs on to customers.

headline: Broadcom shares plunge after AI forecast disappoints
summary: STORY: The AI boom isn’t boosting all chip stocks equally.Shares in Broadcom plunged as much as 13% Wednesday after its outlook for demand disappointed investors.The U.S. firm said it expects AI chip revenue of $16 billion in the third quarter, a little below expectations.Revenue for the previous period also fell short of Wall Street hopes.Chief Executive Hock Tan said Broadcom was leaving its long-range forecast for AI chip sales unchanged at $100 billion.The firm’s ability to meet demand has been strained by supply chain issues.It also faces a growing threat from rivals like Marvell Technology, which is making inroads with sales to tech giants.Even so, Broadcom remains a major beneficiary of the AI investment race.Analysts say it has a strong position in custom chips.They are in hot demand amid the rise of inference - the process by which chatbots respond to user queries.Big Tech firms like Alphabet and Microsoft are expected to spend some $700 billion on AI infrastructure this year, with much of that money set to flow to chipmakers.
